Where there is a white Rohltaka tree surrounded by an ant-
hill, there will be water at a depth of 350 cubits and one cubit to
the east.

516 Brhat Sarhhild
^ u^u
If there is a white Sami tree full of thorns, there will be
water at depth of 375 cubits and one cubit to the south of the tree.
At a depth of 2J cubits there will be a snake.
[Thus the author refers to a number of desert trees with or with-
out ant-hills in their vicinity as the guiding factors locating water.
In modern times tube wells are sunk to get water from very great
depths. Here the maximum depth in desert areas is 375 cubits and
the minimum is 25 cubits, while elsewhere they are 37J cubits and
7J cubits respectively.]

The existence of water in a sero^-desert area (where there is
little water) should not be predicted on such symptoms as are
found in a desert.
